item.title: FirstFT: US strikes Iranian missile sites
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: shrinking real wages and bank deregulation

item.title: AI and the brave new world of deals
item.description: Global M&A is now dominated by the race to control the world’s energy, fibre networks and compute

item.title: SpaceX to drive a cybertruck through corporate governance norms
item.description: Provisions in the IPO prospectus will give Elon Musk more freedom — and potentially less accountability

item.title: AI stories aren’t inevitably ‘not art’
item.description: Exercising our own judgment when it comes to quality is something we should not outsource to machines

item.title: Spotify chief defends AI-generated music
item.description: Streaming app strikes deal with Universal allowing subscribers to create ‘controlled’ covers and remixes

item.title: Major European airlines exposed to €1.5bn-plus in carbon costs, analysis shows
item.description: Extension of emissions trading scheme opposed by flag carriers

item.title: Monzo spends heavily on ‘refer a friend’ rewards to boost market share
item.description: London-based fintech records ‘staggering’ increase in spending on referral fees

item.title: Deregulation hands top US and UK banks $1.3tn opening
item.description: Regulatory loosening has allowed countries’ banks to expand balance sheets while EU and Swiss rivals are constrained

item.title: Top European wind turbine maker calls ‘non-western’ rivals a security threat
item.description: Nordex CEO José Luis Blanco says region’s ‘supply chain independence’ must be protected

item.title: Call for entries: Europe’s Leading Start-up Hubs 2027
item.description: We are inviting business incubator and accelerator schemes to take part in the fourth edition of our ranking

item.title: Apple has an innovation gap. Will its new CEO fill it?
item.description: The tech titan that once urged consumers to ‘think different’ may itself be ripe for disruption in the AI age

item.title: There are more important things to consider when moving countries than tax
item.description: Relocating to a low-tax jurisdiction often involves unforeseen legal, emotional and social costs

item.title: The predatory advance of surveillance pricing
item.description: Extracting the maximum possible surplus by using personalised data should not be legal

item.title: Bond Street’s loss is Sloane Street’s gain in London’s luxury retail landscape
item.description: Amid a decline in tourist spending in Mayfair, the high number of wealthy local shoppers is luring fashion brands to Chelsea

item.title: The UK’s best-performing bank is probably not the one you think
item.description: Shares in London-listed but Georgia-based Lion Finance have risen 800% since 2021

item.title: China’s change in maths on carbon emissions masks growth, report says
item.description: Independent analysis suggests retrospective change to data indicating climate change progress

item.title: Reeves urges ministers to ‘buy British’ in critical sectors
item.description: UK chancellor aims to prioritise domestic procurement in shipbuilding, steel, AI and energy infrastructure

item.title: Ferrari reborn: a legend goes electric
item.description: Meet the Luce – Jony Ive and Marc Newson’s ‘molto disruptive’ hypercar

item.title: Ferrari launches first EV with Jony Ive’s ‘polarising’ design
item.description: Ex-Apple designer aims to help Italian luxury brand win over a younger demographic with €550,000 futuristic ‘Luce’ model

item.title: The energy shock is not over yet
item.description: Even with a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, supplies will take time to normalise

item.title: The 19th-century guide to running an effective meeting
item.description: A US officer and engineer devised the process that is still in use today

item.title: Counting the cost of New York’s sky-high food bills
item.description: Spiralling prices could lead to more public programmes

item.title: Two LNG tankers pass through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Another vessel carrying crude oil crossed the vital shipping lane on Saturday

item.title: Musk, SpaceX and the world’s most far-out IPO
item.description: His ambition is to privatise the heavens — and to become the world’s first trillionaire

item.title: Italian bank CDP to raise stake in payments group Nexi after CVC weighs bid
item.description: Cassa Depositi e Prestiti’s move comes as buyout firm had considered €9bn bid for Milan-listed group

item.title: Pope Leo warns AI revolution driven by ‘idolatry of profit’
item.description: Pontiff says it is ‘not permissible’ to entrust lethal decisions to artificial systems

item.title: Prize draw businesses have better luck than the average punter
item.description: Lack of regulation has helped draw operators to thrive

item.title: AI guardrails stripped from Meta and Google models in minutes
item.description: Software designed to remove safety protections creates systems that provide responses on biological weapons and malware

item.title: Singapore tells banks to speed up account openings for wealthy clients
item.description: Regulator tries to strengthen city-state’s position as wealth hub after money-laundering cases lengthened wait times

item.title: Inside Chris Hohn’s Top Hedge Fund
item.description: Plus, James Anderson sees end to tech boom, SpaceX IPO trading frenzy and the Old Vic’s all-female Glengarry Glen Ross
